CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Continued To Feb-19-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Individual Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jonathan Blavin, Esq. 415-512-4011 of Munger Tolles & Olson LLP for Defendant LYFT.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S Motion To Compel Individual Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration.

Defendant Lyft Inc.'s motion to compel individual arbitration and stay proceedings pending arbitration is granted. The assertion by Plaintiff Allyson McGee that she does not recall reading or signing the Terms and Conditions and the arbitration agreement contained therein is not a basis to avoid enforcing the agreement. (See Cox v. Bonni (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 287, 301 ["when a person with capacity of reading and understanding an instrument signs it, he may not, in the absence of fraud, imposition or excusable neglect, avoid its terms on the ground he failed to read it before signing it."]; Bolanos v. Khalatian (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 1586, 1590 [enforcing arbitration agreement despite plaintiff declaring that she did "not remember signing it"].) (see word minutes for full details)

A proposed order is submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant Lyft, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Individual Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration [For Tentative Ruling Only]

A Proposed order is submitted by email.
